context: Tests whether the agent correctly sets up a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for an npm TypeScript library using semantic-release, covering the verify/release job structure, concurrency configuration, [skip ci] guards, checkout depth, bot identity, permissions, and semantic-release plugin ordering and configuration.

checklist.name: fetch-depth verify
checklist.description: The verify job's checkout step uses `fetch-depth: 0` (not the default shallow clone)

checklist.name: fetch-depth release
checklist.description: The release job's checkout step uses `fetch-depth: 0`

checklist.name: Verify concurrency group
checklist.description: The verify job (or workflow-level) has a cancellable concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: true`

checklist.name: Release concurrency group
checklist.description: The release job has a job-level non-cancellable concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: false`

checklist.name: skip ci on verify
checklist.description: The verify job has an `if:` condition that skips execution when the commit message contains `[skip ci]`

checklist.name: skip ci on release
checklist.description: The release job has an `if:` condition that skips execution when the commit message contains `[skip ci]`

checklist.name: Bot identity in step env
checklist.description: GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, GIT_COMMITTER_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL are set inside the semantic-release step's `env:`, NOT at job level

checklist.name: Bot uses noreply address
checklist.description: The bot email addresses use a `noreply.github.com` domain (not a real human email)

checklist.name: Release permissions
checklist.description: The release job explicitly declares only the needed write permissions, including `contents: write` for GitHub release/pushback and `id-token: write` for npm Trusted Publishing; `issues: write` and `pull-requests: write` are absent unless comments are configured

checklist.name: semantic-release action version
checklist.description: Uses `cycjimmy/semantic-release-action` pinned to a full commit SHA with an exact same-line version comment, preserving the repo's current major unless there is a concrete migration reason

checklist.name: Plugin order
checklist.description: Plugins are ordered: commit-analyzer before release-notes-generator, both before publish plugins, @semantic-release/git before @semantic-release/github

checklist.name: git plugin message
checklist.description: The @semantic-release/git plugin's message contains `[skip ci]` and uses the `chore(release): ${nextRelease.version}` format

checklist.name: No registry token auth
checklist.description: The semantic-release npm publish path uses npm Trusted Publishing/OIDC and does not set `registry-url` or expose `NPM_TOKEN`

checklist.name: release needs verify
checklist.description: The release job declares `needs: [verify]` (or equivalent), making verify the gate to publish

checklist.name: GitHub token on step
checklist.description: GITHUB_TOKEN or the dedicated release-bot token is declared only on the semantic-release step, not at the job level

checklist.name: Checkout credential boundary
checklist.description: Checkout/install/build steps do not expose persisted write credentials before package-manager lifecycle scripts run; any pushback credential is introduced only at the release boundary

checklist.name: npm package metadata
checklist.description: package.json includes public `repository` metadata matching the GitHub repo and public `publishConfig.access` when publishing a scoped public package
